layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-REVISED
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE LAYOUT. The slide is structured as a single dominant table container spanning the full usable slide area below the title. The table uses a multi-column grid structure with a fixed left column for workstream names, a middle column for task details, a third column for owner assignments, and four right columns representing timeline quarters (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027). The layout follows a formal regulatory table pattern (Flat Geometry Table, Design Guidelines Section 4 Option C) with no filled header bars, using accent tabs beneath column headers and dashed grey separator lines between rows. Content is distributed evenly across the vertical space with all rows sharing identical heights to maintain horizontal alignment. The table fills the slide confidently with minimal padding, appropriate for dense structured content. A thin brand-color border wraps the entire table container with subtle rounded corners and an even light grey shadow glow for card lift. The slide contains ALL FIVE workstreams from the source data (i through v) with their complete task row sets, totaling approximately 18-20 task rows distributed evenly across the table height. Font sizing is optimized to prevent text overflow in dense multi-line cells while maintaining within-slide uniformity.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.1, 0.9]

Containers:
  C0 (SLIDE TITLE AREA): Top-aligned title area spanning full slide width. Contains the main slide heading 'Fund Accounting platform initiative workplan' in heading font, brand primary color, left-aligned. Height constrained to maximum 10% of slide area. Clear vertical gap below the title before the table container begins. No background fill, no border. The yellow annotation box visible in the source slide ('@Ponmalar: please format to fit the content on the page') is removed entirely — it is editorial markup, not content to be redesigned. The 'Current focus' label visible in the source is also removed as it is a source slide artifact, not structured content.
  C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): Dominant table container filling all remaining slide space below the title with a small top margin (approximately 2-3% of slide height). Structured as a Flat Geometry Table (Design Guidelines Section 4 Option C) — formal, regulatory-appropriate pattern with no filled header bars, suitable for project planning and workstream tracking content. The table uses a flex row structure with seven columns: (1) Workstream name column (narrow, approximately 12% width), (2) Details column (wider, approximately 20% width to accommodate multi-line task descriptions), (3) Owner(s) column (medium, approximately 16% width), (4-7) Four timeline columns for 2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027 (equal-peers, sharing remaining width split four ways, approximately 13% each). All columns are flex columns internally. HEADER ROW TREATMENT: Each column header is plain bold text in body font, brand dark color, left-aligned with small left inset. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary fill, very thin height — approximately 3-4% of column width, narrow width relative to column — approximately 25-30% of column width, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned, positioned fully below the text without overlap). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line (brand dark color, approximately 1pt weight) spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns (not a single full-width line). CONTENT ROWS STRUCTURE: The table contains ALL FIVE workstreams from the source slide: (i) Conduct discovery with 3 task rows (Define high-level business requirements, Design target state architecture, Design target state data flows), (ii) Select and onboard partner with 3 task rows (Evaluate and select fund accounting platform partner, Assess if 3rd-party consulting support is required, Negotiate and finalize contract(s)), (iii) Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC with 1 task row (Configure and test the system, conduct parallel runs and go-live on the new platform), (iv) Implement fund accounting platform - Holdings with 1 task row (Configure and test the system, conduct parallel runs and go-live on the new platform), (v) Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments with 1 task row (Configure and test the system, conduct parallel runs and go-live on the new platform). This yields approximately 9 distinct task rows total. Each task row spans all seven columns and all task rows share identical height via equal flexGrow values to maintain perfect horizontal alignment across all columns. ROW SEPARATORS: Thin dashed grey horizontal lines (light grey color, dashed pattern, approximately 1pt weight) between every task row, spanning full table width from left edge to right edge, positioned precisely at row boundaries. No separator after the final row. WORKSTREAM COLUMN TREATMENT: Workstream identifiers (i., ii., iii., iv., v.) are bold text, brand dark color, left-aligned with small left inset (approximately 2-3% of column width), vertically centered within their merged row span. Each workstream identifier cell is merged vertically to span all task rows belonging to that workstream. For workstream i (3 rows), ii (3 rows), iii (1 row), iv (1 row), v (1 row), the cell merge spans the appropriate number of task rows. DETAILS COLUMN TREATMENT: Each task detail is plain body text, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically top-aligned within its row to accommodate multi-line content. Text wraps naturally within the column width. Examples: 'Define high-level business requirements', 'Design target state architecture', 'Evaluate and select fund accounting platform partner', 'Configure and test the system, conduct parallel runs and go-live on the new platform'. OWNER(S) COLUMN TREATMENT: Owner assignments use a two-part structure: role prefix (Finance/Ops:, Tech:, TO:) in bold brand dark color, followed by names in regular weight brand text color. Multiple names separated by commas. Text left-aligned with small left inset, vertically top-aligned within the row to accommodate multi-line content. Examples: 'Finance/Ops: Katarina (with input from Tyson, Moira/George, Tom, Therese, Patrick, Tiffany)', 'Tech: Devang, Leon, Dan R', 'TO: Justine, Amy'. TIMELINE COLUMNS (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027) TREATMENT: Deliverable text for each task, body font, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically top-aligned within the row. Bullet points rendered as native PowerPoint bullets with brand-color bullet markers. Multi-line bullet lists are common in these columns (particularly 2026 Q2 which contains the densest content with 3-4 bullet points per cell in some rows). Examples from 2026 Q2: 'Draft high-level business requirements • Prioritize requirements by must-have and nice-to-have • Align on requirements and prioritization', 'Define and align on the role of the fund accounting platform (e.g., where the sub-ledger will reside) in Antares ecosystem', 'Develop RFP • Finalize partner shortlist, issue RFP and facilitate Q&A • Design and conduct POC • Finalize evaluation framework'. Rows with no deliverable content for a given quarter remain empty (white fill, no text). CONTAINER STYLING: White fill, thin brand primary border (approximately 1-2pt weight), visibly rounded corners (subtle radius, approximately 3-4% of container width, not sharp), even light grey shadow glow for card lift effect (subtle, no hard drop shadow). Internal padding: minimal on all sides (approximately 2-3% of container dimensions) — dense content requires tight padding per Beauty Principle 0.1. The table confidently fills the available slide space. FONT SIZING OPTIMIZATION (CRITICAL FIX): All body text within the table uses the same font size (within-slide uniformity, Rule C6). Font size target: small range (9-10pt) to accommodate the densest multi-line cells without text overflow or clipping. This is a REDUCTION from the previous attempt's font size to eliminate the severe text overflow observed in the 2026 Q2 column and Owner(s) column. Column headers use the same size as body text or slightly larger, bold weight. The reduced font size is applied uniformly to all body text cells: Workstream names, Details text, Owner names, and all Timeline deliverable text. ROW HEIGHT OPTIMIZATION: After applying the reduced font size, row heights are recalculated to distribute the available vertical space evenly across all 9 task rows. Each row receives equal flexGrow value. The row height is sized to comfortably contain the densest cells (those with 3-4 bullet points) at the reduced font size without overflow, while also avoiding excessive whitespace in single-line cells. ALIGNMENT PRECISION: All task rows across all seven columns share identical top_pct and height_pct values. Dashed separator lines appear at identical vertical positions across all columns. This is a hard structural constraint (Blueprint Step 0.5c). Workstream identifier cells in column 1 are vertically merged to span their respective task row sets, with the text vertically centered within the merged cell span. CONTENT FIDELITY VERIFICATION: The layout explicitly includes all five workstreams (i, ii, iii, iv, v) with their complete task row sets as specified in the source slide. Workstream v ('Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments') is present with its task row ('Configure and test the system, conduct parallel runs and go-live on the new platform') and associated owners ('Finance/Ops: Therese, Tiffany, Katarina, Tyson' and 'Tech: Devang') and timeline deliverables ('Leveraging the Evolution equities work • Conduct data mapping & conversion, system configuration and integration build • Conduct QA and UAT • Conduct parallel runs before cutover'). All task rows from the source slide are accounted for in this layout description.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide is well-filled by the dominant table container which spans approximately 90% of the slide area. No bottom decorative layer is needed. Adding one would violate the accent color budget (Rule C3) and provide no visual grouping benefit. The table container's own border and shadow provide sufficient visual definition.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide contains no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. All content is self-contained within the single table structure. No elements span across containers or require top-layer rendering.
